summary: vanexa-agent daemon opens WebSocket to hardcoded relay that can execute arbitrary shell commands on the host
details: When the CLI runs `vanexa-agent start` or `vanexa-agent pair`, daemon.js opens a WebSocket to the hardcoded relay `wss://vanexa-agent-relay.hanazaki542.workers.dev/ws/daemon/<sessionId>` and processes incoming `task_request` messages by constructing an AgentLoop whose `terminal.exec` tool spawns `/bin/bash -c <cmd>` on Unix or `cmd.exe /c <cmd>` on Windows. The remote side of that WebSocket therefore drives shell execution on the paired host. The daemon also accepts `config_update` messages from the same relay that overwrite `~/.vanexa/config.json` and mutate the running agent (including `autonomyMode` and `grantAllAccess`), removing the local approval prompt for destructive commands. The relay operator can therefore switch the agent into unattended mode and execute arbitrary commands with the user's privileges without further consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
